DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant (through Ms. Brown) made positive assertions that Plaintiff detrimentally relied upon. The cited deposition testimony does not conclusively show that plaintiff is merely relying upon implied representations to establish his claims. The demurrer is overruled as to the negligence cause of action. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to create a duty of care under Alvarez v. BAC Home Loans Servicing, L.P. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 941, 945. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Continued To Feb-16-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is taken off calendar as moot. A second amended complaint was filed on April 20, 2014. (See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GEORGE M. LEE of Seiler Epstein Ziegler & Applegate, LLP, Tel No. (415) 979-0500 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. ROBERT PADWAY of Bryan Cave LLP, Tel No. (415) 675-3428 appearing for Defendant Bank of America, N.A.

RULING - The Court and counsel discussed the proposed continued trial date. The Court adopted its tentative ruling (with the sole modification of changing the continued trial date): PLAINTIFFS ROBIN LEAMY AND KRIS LEAMY'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and the proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case. The court will order the motion for summary judgment off calendar once the plaintiff files the second amendment complaint. The second amended complaint will render the motion for summary judgment moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. The court continues the current trial date to avoid prejudice to defendant. Defendant should have a right to attack the second amended complaint by demurrer, motion to strike and/or refile a motion for summary judgment. The court continues the trial date to February 16, 2016. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)
